name: Savory Choucroute
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds sauerkraut, plastic bag not can
2 cups beef stock, or more if needed
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour off the brine from sauerkraut and rinse. Drain thoroughly.
2. Squeeze out all water from the sauerkraut.
3. Cut bacon into pieces and cook in one cup of water, simmering about 10 minutes.
4. Drain the cooked bacon.
5. Melt butter in a three-quart, non-aluminum casserole over medium heat.
6. Add bacon, carrots, and onions to the casserole and cook slowly for 10 minutes.
7. Mix in the sauerkraut and cook an additional 10 minutes.
8. Add parsley, peppercorns, bay leaves, gin, wine, and stock to the casserole. Mix well and add salt.
9. Preheat the oven to 300°F.
10. Butter a piece of waxed paper large enough to cover the sauerkraut.
11. Place the buttered side down in the pan and press it into the casserole.
12. Bring to a simmer, cover with the waxed paper, and bake in the oven for 4 hours and 30 minutes.
13. Prick the sausages and brown lightly in a skillet.
14. Remove the cover and waxed paper from the casserole and add the sausages to the kraut (without rendered fat).
15. Continue baking in the oven for another 30 minutes or until liquid is absorbed.
